title: Scrum/Standup/Daily Update
summary: The standup covered dealer email issues, Copilot updates, and voicemail feature development. Dealer email problems remain unresolved, while Copilot's initial version was updated for stability. A personalized voicemail feature for Premier Collective was developed, and the team decided to spike out a real-time text-to-speech generation approach.

decisions.description: Start the meeting after waiting a couple more minutes for Matt Watson and Herender.
Spike out the real-time text-to-speech generation approach for the personalized voicemail feature to avoid generating unnecessary recordings and to handle updated customer names.
Evaluate real-time generation options and incorporate the evaluation as a subtask in the current card.
Discuss whether to cancel the Thursday standup during tomorrow's or Wednesday's meeting when more participants are present.

actionItems.task: Compile and send the different secret shop requirements to Sergey Lyapustin.

expectedOutcome.description: Sergey will receive documentation outlining all requirements from the manufacturer for the secret shop task.

actionItems.task: Schedule a conversation with the momentum API team about their API topic.

expectedOutcome.description: A meeting will be scheduled with the momentum API team for tomorrow or Wednesday morning.

actionItems.task: Schedule a call with John to review the infrastructure and potentially test one more integration with the elite weekly task.

expectedOutcome.description: A call will be scheduled with John tomorrow morning to review the infrastructure and test the elite weekly task integration.

actionItems.task: Deploy the conserver side to production and test it for the finished feature in the portal for the conditional tags.

expectedOutcome.description: The conserver side will be deployed to production, and the conditional tags feature will be fully tested.

actionItems.task: Rewrite the tag for detecting when an agent starts to leave a voicemail and the customer answers, ensuring it is valid before setting up any alerting on those calls.

expectedOutcome.description: The voicemail tag will be rewritten to be more accurate, and its validity will be confirmed before alerting is set up.

actionItems.task: Bring up the issue of 'unknown last name' being included in the voicemail to Kan Sing to see if they can use LLM to filter out those names.

expectedOutcome.description: Kan Sing will evaluate the possibility of using LLM to filter out 'unknown' last names from voicemails.

actionItems.task: Listen to a few voicemails from last week to see if the return telephone number appearance issue has gotten better.

expectedOutcome.description: Kevin will verify if the return telephone number speed issue in voicemails has been resolved based on recent calls.

actionItems.task: Make some quick research to get to evaluate the real-time text-to-speech cost for generating voicemails.

expectedOutcome.description: Sergey will conduct quick research on estimating the real-time text-to-speech generation costs.

tensions: Concern about potentially losing dealers if the email issue is not fixed soon.
Sergey Lyapustin raised concerns about pre-generating customer names for the personalized voicemail feature, as names might be updated later.
Matt Watson cautioned that introducing AI increases the variable for mistakes in the personalized voicemail feature.
The Sophia Flores placeholder name used in the current beta voicemail implementation caused some confusion.
Kevin Breen brought up the automated voicemail leaving only a first name and the return telephone number being too fast in the voicemail.
referencesToPast: Reference to a meeting just before the standup to discuss potential alternative approaches for the personalized voicemail feature.
Mention of an email thread regarding the momentum API topic.
Kevin Breen brought up two older voicemail issues.
